page_name: Yoga Course Details — Online 300 Hour Yoga Teacher Training — RYT 300
rationale: An almost-complete page already exists at apps/web/src/app/yoga-courses/online-300-hour-ytt/page.tsx using every existing section. Diff vs Figma decomposition yields only TWO real gaps: (a) Testimonials section is missing from the page AND TestimonialsSection lacks the Figma mosaic layout (1 lg + 4 sm), (b) CourseHeroSection has no support for the 'Available in 4 Centers — Check availability' strip seen in Figma. Both component changes are STRICTLY ADDITIVE (new opt-in props, defaults preserved) per the shared-components guardrail. Everything else is content fine-tuning at the page level.

demo_page.reason: An existing production page already renders these sections. Per orchestrator instruction: extend the EXISTING page, do not create /demo/* — visual-qa screenshots /yoga-courses/online-300-hour-ytt directly.

parallel_groups.G1: Component extensions — testimonials-section.tsx + course-hero-section.tsx (independent files, run in parallel)
parallel_groups.G2: Page integration — depends on both G1 tasks

tasks.summary: Add additive opt-in `layout='mosaic'` prop + per-item `size: 'lg' | 'sm'` to TestimonialsSection. Default 'grid' behaviour must remain pixel-identical (this component is consumed on 5+ pages).

new_props.name: layout
new_props.notes: When 'mosaic' is set, render a non-uniform CSS-grid: one item with size:'lg' spans 2 rows on lg breakpoints; remaining size:'sm' items take a single row each. Use grid-template-rows + row-span utilities (e.g. lg:grid-rows-2 with the lg card row-span-2). On mobile/tablet, mosaic collapses to a 1- or 2-column vertical stack — same as the default grid.

extended_type.TestimonialItem: add optional `size?: 'lg' | 'sm'` — only consumed when layout='mosaic'

preserved: All existing default-grid styling, motion variants, viewport config, eyebrow/heading typography
Existing consumers (homepage, retreats, etc.) must compile and render identically with NO code change
implementation_hints: Branch on `layout`: build two render paths inside the same component to keep the public surface tight.
For mosaic on lg+: `grid lg:grid-cols-3 lg:grid-rows-2 gap-6` and apply `lg:row-span-2 lg:col-span-1` to the size:'lg' card. Mobile + md: revert to existing `grid gap-6 md:grid-cols-2`.
Keep the existing stagger/reveal variants — they apply to any layout.
TestimonialCard accepts the same data shape; only the wrapper layout changes. Do NOT modify TestimonialCard.

If the size:'lg' card needs visually-different padding/quote size, add it via the TestimonialCard's existing `showDecorativeQuote` prop or pass a `className` only on the lg wrapper — do NOT introduce a new TestimonialCard variant.

interaction.notes: Existing component already implements scroll-reveal + stagger. Verify the existing stagger value (currently 0.1s) matches the 'stagger-children-slow' (0.12s) primitive — bump to 0.12 if drift is desired but NOT required.

acceptance: Adding `layout` to the prop type does NOT break any existing import (it is optional with default 'grid').
When layout='grid' (default), the rendered DOM is byte-identical to current output — verified by running existing pages (homepage, retreats) before/after.
When layout='mosaic' with one size:'lg' + four size:'sm' items, the lg card visually occupies a tall slot (row-span-2) at lg breakpoints; sm cards fill the remaining grid cells.
Mosaic collapses to a stacked single-column layout at mobile and a 2-column layout at md, with no overlap or clipping.
Motion (scroll reveal + stagger + hover-lift) still fires in mosaic layout.

contract.summary: Add additive opt-in `availability` prop to CourseHeroSection rendering the 'Available in 4 Centers  ·  Check availability →' strip seen in Figma between the meta-chips and the CTA. Strip is OPTIONAL; when omitted, nothing renders (shared-component default preserved).

new_props.name: availability
new_props.type: { label: string; linkLabel?: string; linkHref?: string } | undefined
new_props.notes: label = 'Available in 4 Centers'. linkLabel + linkHref render the trailing 'Check availability' link. If linkHref omitted, render as plain text. When the prop itself is omitted, the strip is not rendered (existing pages unaffected).

preserved: All existing default rendering, motion timing, breadcrumb/string union, image dimensions
The current 300-hour page passes no `availability` — its visual output must be unchanged after this extension lands

implementation_hints:
Insert the strip in the left column BETWEEN the subtitle

and the CTA — that matches the Figma vertical order (title → subtitle → availability strip → CTA → meta-chips).
Style: small horizontal flex row, divider dot in between, eyebrow-style mini text on the left, `hover-underline` tailwind primitive on the link.
Tokens: `text-mini text-text-tertiary` for label; `text-mini text-text-brand` + `hover:underline` for link.
Animate this row with fade-in-up at delay ~0.45 (between subtitle 0.3 and CTA 0.55) so it fits the existing mount stagger.
Honor reduced-motion the same way the existing motion blocks do.
acceptance: Omitting `availability` produces zero DOM output for the strip — the 300-hour page is visually unchanged until T3 wires the prop.
Passing `availability={{label, linkLabel, linkHref}}` renders the strip between subtitle and CTA with a divider/dot separator.
Link uses next/link and navigates to linkHref; no link rendered when linkHref is undefined.
Strip mount animation fires at delay≈0.45 in the existing stagger; reduced-motion bypasses transition.
tokens used: text-mini, text-text-tertiary, text-text-brand — no hardcoded hex / clamp().

contract.summary: Wire the two extended components into the existing page: pass `availability` prop to CourseHeroSection and INSERT a new TestimonialsSection (layout='mosaic') between Certification and PreRequisites — matching the Figma y-coordinate ordering.

edits.what: Add `import { TestimonialsSection } from "@/components/sections/testimonials-section";` near the other section imports.
Add to <CourseHeroSection /> a new prop: `availability={{ label: 'Available in 4 Centers', linkLabel: 'Check availability', linkHref: '/centers' }}`.
edits.rationale: Mirrors Figma copy. linkHref defaults to /centers per the interaction spec's best-guess.

edits.what: Optional: keep current breadcrumb string OR change to BreadcrumbItem[] to match Figma's 3-crumb chain (Home / Yoga courses / Online 300 Hour Yoga Teacher Training). Recommended: switch to array so the third crumb (current page) appears.
edits.rationale: Figma shows three crumbs; current page passes a 2-segment string.

edits.what: Insert <RevealOnScroll as="section" id="testimonials" className="scroll-mt-[160px]"> containing <TestimonialsSection layout="mosaic" eyebrow="Your Guide" heading="What People Are Saying?" testimonials={[...]}/> between the </section> closing the Certification banner and the <section id="pre-requisites"> opener.

data_items.quote: If I could tell my past self that I'd someday quit packaged food, she'd laugh—chips were my weakness! I can now distinguish what's good for my body and treat it like a temple, not a trash can.

If you're unsure, just attend—it could change your life! You live only once, so if you want to enjoy your life in the true sense, just go enroll yourself into this workshop. It could add ten years to your life!
If I could tell my past self that I'd someday quit packaged food, she'd laugh—chips were my weakness! I can now distinguish what's good for my body and treat it like a temple, not a trash can.

edits.what: (Optional) Update CourseSectionNav 'Overall' tab href to '#testimonials' OR add a sixth 'Testimonials' tab. Current page maps 'Overall' to '#instructors'. Per interaction spec, 'Overall' is unresolved; safest to leave as-is OR add an explicit Testimonials anchor and keep 'Overall' pointing to closing-cta. Recommend: NO change to tabs in this pass to keep blast radius minimal.

page_level.concern: scroll-mt-[160px] on the new #testimonials section so the sticky CourseSectionNav doesn't hide the heading on anchor jumps.
RevealOnScroll wrapper triggers the section's existing scroll-into-view reveal. TestimonialsSection already has its own motion — RevealOnScroll just gates the boundary.

acceptance: Page compiles with no TS errors; tsc + next build clean.
CourseHeroSection renders the 'Available in 4 Centers · Check availability' strip between subtitle and CTA.
The new Testimonials section appears between Certification and PreRequisites in DOM order, matching Figma y-coords.
Testimonials renders with layout='mosaic' — one tall card + four short cards on lg breakpoints.
All previously rendered sections still render in the same order with the same content.
No edits to TestimonialCard, ChecklistItem, ProgramCard, or any other shared primitive in this task.

contract.summary: Visual + functional QA pass against Figma 805:10091.
checks: Run dev server, visit /yoga-courses/online-300-hour-ytt, scroll the full page.
Confirm hero shows 'Available in 4 Centers · Check availability →' strip below the subtitle.
Confirm Testimonials section sits between Certification and PreRequisites; mosaic layout visible at >=lg.
Click 'Check availability' link — navigates to /centers (or whatever prop value is wired).
Verify nothing on home / retreats / other testimonial consumers regressed (default 'grid' layout still pixel-identical).
Run `nx build web` and `tsc --noEmit` — both clean.
Reduced-motion check: enable prefers-reduced-motion in browser; entrances should resolve instantly.

out_of_scope: Recreating any existing section as a 'new' component — all 24 reuse-as-is matches stay as-is.
Modifying TestimonialCard, ChecklistItem, ProgramCard, InstructorCard, or any other ui/* primitive — none need changes.
Adding a /demo/* route — visual-qa screenshots the live page route instead.
Wiring the 'Reserve Your Spot Now' modal flow — flagged unresolved in 02_interactions.json; defer.
Filling in missing FAQ answers — current page already has 4 complete FAQs; Figma had blanks.

Adding new icon keys to highlights-section.tsx — current page already uses the existing keys (yoga, leaf, strength) instead of the Figma's literal keys (yoga-meets-flight, leaf-filled, build-strength). The remapping is already done; no further work.
open_questions: Confirm Testimonials eyebrow: Figma copy is 'Your Guide' (likely a Figma copy-paste error from Instructors). Plan ships Figma-literal value 'Your Guide' but recommend updating to 'Testimonials' with design.
Confirm 'Check availability' destination — guessed /centers per interaction spec. Stake-holder may want /yoga-courses/online-300-hour-ytt#centers anchor instead.
Confirm whether CourseSectionNav should gain a 6th 'Testimonials' tab. Currently no-op.
